message:
fix(console): handle backslash-escaped brackets in strip_markup

Add backslash escape handling to strip_markup so that \[ and \] produce
literal brackets in plain output, matching Rich's escaping convention.
Previously, \[OK\] would strip [OK] as a markup tag, producing broken
"tools/list \ 12ms" instead of "tools/list [OK] 12ms".

console.rs:
- Add '\' case in strip_markup parser: \[ → [, \] → ], \\ → \
- Add test_strip_markup_preserves_backslash_escaped_brackets with 3 cases

repro_markup.rs:
- Update reproduction test to assert correct behavior instead of
  documenting the previously broken behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
